# Evidence Pack: Strongest Commercially Viable Restaurant Direct-Channel Offer

**Decision supported:** Determine the smallest useful sellable offer, logical package ladder, bundle/add-on boundaries, segment fit, recurring-responsibility implications, safe promises, and pricing-model direction without final public prices.
**Created:** 2026-06-13
**Query budget used:** 6 conservatively counted attempts / 25; 19 remaining
**Notebook boundaries used/excluded:** Selected all four boundaries because each existing request cluster maps to one distinct registered notebook. ER-001 through ER-004 all succeeded in recorded order. No cross-notebook query, deep research, source import, or account switch was used.

## Executive Evidence Summary

All four existing evidence requests returned source-grounded answers: 197 numbered citations mapped to 77 notebook source references across the four responses. The evidence is sufficient for bounded advisor memos because it covers restaurant-side value/risks, operations, alternatives, coexistence, onboarding/support, customization pressure, and generic provider-retention boundaries. It is not sufficient for economic promises, final packages/prices, or a commercial verdict because incremental conversion/payback, observed SLA performance, migration rates, and UAE provider unit economics remain unquantified.

## Claims

| ID | Claim | Classification | Geography | Citation/provenance | Limitation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| E-001 | Aggregators handle roughly 75% of mobile orders in key UAE/Saudi markets; owned channels/call centers handle about 25%. | Vendor claim | UAE/GCC | ER-001 [3,4], sources `f478fdb2-f0da-4a1d-be8b-218d380a2ad5`, `f0e64d6b-9bae-4db1-a00f-6f536d11ac59` | Provider/industry-report scope unclear. |
| E-003 | Timely delivery and order accuracy are primary retention determinants in a UAE young-adult study. | Verified fact | UAE | ER-001 [15,21,23], source `ca789d1f-d041-4a5c-bcc2-b392dd3c80b0` | Convenience sample, N=100, ages 18-25. |
| E-005 | Aggregator commissions/fees compress margins and operators object to lost customer data/control. | Verified fact | GCC | ER-001 [4,33,39-41], sources `f0e64d6b-9bae-4db1-a00f-6f536d11ac59`, `928134b3-f8ac-49a6-b83f-09a3efa5aaa4` | Contract terms vary; some evidence is older. |
| E-008 | Owned-channel orders can integrate with POS/order management, dispatch, tracking, and status updates. | Vendor claim | UAE | ER-002 [1-3], sources `c62b5759-f7e9-4aff-a4c8-f0cdecfa15a1`, `0b777f80-18e6-4d54-86bb-500624588e90` | Capability does not establish restaurant readiness. |
| E-010 | Staging and webhook status flows permit technical validation before live delivery. | Verified fact | generic SaaS/UAE | ER-002 [39-41], sources `772eb845-9e0b-4edd-8bc4-ced67dfb93f0`, `87f2db35-6a61-47d0-b110-453a57ccce3b` | Technical gate only. |
| E-011 | On-time, first-attempt success, replacement latency, and peak performance are candidate pilot gates. | Scenario assumption | UAE | ER-002 [37,38,42-45], five source IDs recorded in evidence ledger | Thresholds are vendor claims. |
| E-014 | Direct-ordering tools expose first-party data/control but require logistics/integration choices. | Vendor claim | UAE/GCC/global | ER-003 [1-5], four source IDs recorded in evidence ledger | Capability does not prove profit. |
| E-017 | POS-integrated SaaS can consolidate channels, inject orders, and synchronize inventory. | Vendor claim | global | ER-003 [23-27,45], four source IDs recorded in evidence ledger | Implementation burden varies. |
| E-019 | Aggregator/direct coexistence and channel consolidation are available strategies. | Vendor claim | UAE/global | ER-003 [42-45], sources recorded in evidence ledger | Successful channel shift is unquantified. |
| E-021 | Standardized rapid launch is possible as a vendor claim, while integration introduces manual setup burden. | Vendor claim | UAE/GCC | ER-004 [8,11-13], sources `673407b7-8f52-4692-9aa3-c15ea2a9b207`, `13a846e1-d097-4937-8097-6d9172be4e6a` | Onboarding labor distribution unavailable. |
| E-023 | Local personalization need conflicts with the scalability benefit of limiting customization. | Contradiction | global | ER-004 [14,36], sources `3e147399-4d42-4b02-a98a-03b1f83e5c9a`, `81996aa5-70fb-4e74-89ec-cad5923c1ba6` | UAE boundary unresolved. |
| E-024 | Low-ARPA/SMB SaaS tends to face higher churn and weaker retention than higher-ARPA models. | Verified fact | generic SaaS | ER-004 [23-25,34,35], sources `022e2828-2807-4ade-9d2f-7b0c7e8ee0ac`, `ae90a697-b5ad-4757-9a7d-b1948e277c1d` | Not a UAE restaurant cohort. |

## Contradictions

E-006: Loyalty-program availability conflicts with low reported consumer subscription/use [50], source `928134b3-f8ac-49a6-b83f-09a3efa5aaa4`.
E-013: Delivery-time expectations differ across providers/service tiers [53,54], sources `2c6fbd2d-0af0-4978-88dc-42b2b3310def`, `a3bd1a72-18ea-43e8-a773-d23b9ee27a99`.
E-018: Direct ordering does not necessarily beat aggregator pricing/convenience despite aggregator commission pressure [30-35,46-48], source IDs recorded in evidence ledger.
E-023: Local personalization need conflicts with limiting customization for repeatability [14,36], sources `3e147399-4d42-4b02-a98a-03b1f83e5c9a`, `81996aa5-70fb-4e74-89ec-cad5923c1ba6`.

## Unresolved Gaps

`GAP-005`: Incremental direct-channel conversion, restaurant payback, and profitable channel-shift rates remain unquantified.

`GAP-006`: UAE provider CAC, onboarding labor, support load, churn, cost-to-serve, and gross margin by segment remain unavailable.

Observed independent-delivery SLA performance and UAE-specific customization boundaries also remain unresolved.

## Narrow Follow-up Requests

No additional query is authorized or necessary for this ordered retrieval stage. Carry `GAP-005` and `GAP-006` into advisor memos as explicit constraints.
